He gets complex partial seizures (deja-vu and orofacial automatsisms) as well as secondary generalised seizures.

Diagnosis Focal epilepsy, probable occipital lobe onset
Impression it does seem that this lady has a drug refractory focal (occipital lobe) epilepsy.
Her normal seizures consist of coloured blobbs in the right side of her version.
She might stare and be unresponsive later for a few minutes.
About once a month she will have a secondary generalised seizure which start like her normal seizure but then will progress to involve loss of consciousness with limb shaking.

Medication:
Zonismaide 100mg bd
Brivitiracetam 50mg bd
